rationale: The citing paper uses Argo, Dahl, and Morales (2008) as part of the core contagion literature it is explicitly building from, not just as a passing background citation. In the theory development, the authors state that prior consumer contagion studies (including 2008) examined purchase-decision effects but had not examined authenticity judgments for everyday brands. The paper then positions its own experiments as addressing that gap, and later discussion again locates its contribution relative to prior positive/negative person-based contagion findings.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Argo et al. (2008) by moving contagion theory from interpersonal retail-contact effects to factory-origin and brand-authenticity judgments. It preserves the contagion line of work but adds a new mechanism context: perceived transfer of brand essence from the original production source.
